jobs.name: Hermes Full Backup — Git sync
jobs.prompt:
Run the Hermes complete backup script (git sync only, no docker images).

Execute:
```
bash /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.sh >> /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log 2>&1
```

The script is at /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.py and backs up all hermes state (config, skills, memories, sessions, state.db, .env, etc.) to the ChonSong/hermes-sync GitHub repo. No ignores, complete backup.

Just run it — no reasoning needed. Log the result to /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log.

jobs.name: Hermes Full Backup — Docker Image
jobs.prompt:
Run the Hermes complete backup script INCLUDING Docker image backup.

Execute:
```
bash /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.sh --full-image >> /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log 2>&1
```

This backs up all hermes state AND saves Docker images (hermes-sync:latest, agent-os, postgres) to /opt/data/backups/docker-images.tar.gz. This takes several minutes and produces a ~5-6GB file that is then included in the next git sync.

Just run it — no reasoning needed.

jobs.name: seans-reporepo refresh
jobs.prompt:
Run the seans-reporepo catalog refresh on the host via SSH.

## Execution
1. Run: ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key sean@172.19.0.1 "bash /home/sean/.hermes/scripts/refresh.sh"
2. If the SSH or script fails → retry up to 2 more times (3 total attempts), waiting 2 minutes between retries
3. If all 3 attempts fail → report failure to user (not just log as error)
4. On success → report what was updated (files changed, new repos discovered, commit SHA)

## Retry logic
- Attempt 1 fails → wait 2 minutes → Attempt 2
- Attempt 2 fails → wait 2 minutes → Attempt 3
- Attempt 3 fails → stop and report failure with error details

Report the output.

jobs.name: context-budget-audit
jobs.prompt:
Run a context budget audit on the Hermes skills system.

## Execution
1. Count all SKILL.md files: find /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/skills -name "SKILL.md" | wc -l
2. Measure total size: du -sh /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/skills/
3. Find the 10 largest skills by file size
4. Report: total count, total size, top 10 largest, and estimated token savings from vendor grouping

Output a clean summary.

jobs.name: hermes-web-computer: canary watch
jobs.prompt:
Run a canary watch check on hermes-web-computer.

The HWC server runs on the **host** at port 3005. From this container, check health via network:

1. Try: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --connect-timeout 5 http://172.19.0.1:3005
2. If that fails, try: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:3005

Report one line:
- "HWC canary: ✅ [timestamp] — port 3005 responding (HTTP 200)"
- "HWC canary: ❌ [timestamp] — port 3005 unreachable (reason)"
- "HWC canary: ⚠️ [timestamp] — HTTP [code] (unexpected status)"

If the server is down, also check if it needs restarting:
- ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key sean@172.19.0.1 "ps aux | grep hwc | grep -v grep"
- If not running and you can, attempt: ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key sean@172.19.0.1 "cd /home/sean/.hermes/hermes-web-computer && nohup ./backend/agent-os server --port 3005 > /tmp/hwc-server.log 2>&1 &"

Do NOT use browser_navigate — the browser tool cannot reach the host server.

jobs.name: hermes-web-computer: rebuild + deploy check
jobs.prompt:
Rebuild and verify hermes-web-computer from the container.

The repo is at `/home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer`. Go is NOT installed in this container, so we use build verification via file checks and git status only — full Go builds happen on the host.

Steps:
1. cd /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer
2. git log --oneline -3
3. git status --short
4. Check frontend build exists: ls -la frontend/dist/ 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo "no frontend/dist"
5. Check server is reachable: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --connect-timeout 5 http://172.19.0.1:3005
6. Count pending commits not on origin: git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline 2>/dev/null | wc -l

Report concisely:
- Git status (commits ahead/behind, dirty files)
- Frontend dist present: yes/no
- Server reachable: HTTP code
- Any issues found

Do NOT try to go build — Go is not installed in this container.

jobs.name: hermes-web-computer: nightly build health
jobs.prompt:
Run build health check for hermes-web-computer.

The HWC project lives at:
- Container path: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer
- Host path: /home/sean/.hermes/hermes-web-computer

Check from container:
1. cd /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer
2. Verify frontend build: test -f frontend/dist/index.html && echo "frontend: OK" || echo "frontend: MISSING"
3. Check git: git log --oneline -1 && git status --short
4. Server health: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --connect-timeout 5 http://172.19.0.1:3005
5. Count e2e test files: find e2e -name "*.spec.ts" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
6. Count backend Go files: find backend -name "*.go" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
7. Count frontend components: find frontend/src/components -name "*.svelte" 2>/dev/null | wc -l

Report one line summary: "HWC health: [date] — frontend: OK/MISSING, server: HTTP[code], e2e: [N] tests, go: [N] files, svelte: [N] components"

If server is unreachable, note it. If frontend is MISSING, note that a rebuild is needed on the host.

jobs.name: html-in-canvas-api-monitor
jobs.prompt:
You are monitoring the Layout Subtree API (HTML-in-Canvas) ecosystem.

Run a check on:
1. **WICG/html-in-canvas** - Any recent commits, issues, or spec changes? Check https://github.com/WICG/html-in-canvas/commits since last run
2. **Chrome status** - Is the flag still required? Any API changes in recent Chrome releases? Check https://chromiumdash.appspot.com/chromium
3. **Remotion docs** - Any updates to HtmlInCanvas component? Check https://www.remotion.dev/docs/remotion/html-in-canvas
4. **Browser support** - Any new browser support (Firefox/Safari)?

Report:
- "STABLE" if API is unchanged and no new browser support
- "UPDATED" if: API changed, new browser support, or flag requirement removed
- "ACTION NEEDED" if: API removed/deprecated, or major breaking change

Keep it brief. One paragraph max.

jobs.name: GTO - Phase 4+5+6 Final Polish
jobs.prompt:
GTO Phase 4+5+6 Polish — Targeted checks.

Do ONE of these per tick (cycle through, skip if done):
4a. TRAINING: grep -r "useQuizApi\|TrainQuiz\|QuizCard" /workspace/gto-wizard-clone/apps/web/src --include="*.tsx" -c (verify quiz components exist)
4b. HH: python3 -c "from apps.api.models.hh_models import HandHistory, SiteEnum; print('HH models OK')" (verify imports — run from /workspace/gto-wizard-clone)
4c. ICM: test -f /workspace/gto-wizard-clone/apps/web/src/app/icm/page.tsx && echo "ICM page OK"
4d. README: grep "✅ Built" /workspace/gto-wizard-clone/README.md | wc -l (should be 7+ lines with ✅)
4e. Verify quiz router exists: test -f /workspace/gto-wizard-clone/apps/api/routers/quiz.py && echo "Quiz router OK"
4f. Check solver tests: cd /workspace/gto-wizard-clone && timeout 30 python3 -m pytest apps/solver/tests/test_cfr.py -v --tb=short -q

After cycling through all items once and confirming nothing needs fixing, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.
If you find a fixable issue, commit and push:
cd /workspace/gto-wizard-clone && git add -A && git commit -m "fix(polish): [desc]" && git push origin main

Do NOT attempt npm/next builds — not available in this context.
Do NOT read/write /opt/data/.env.

jobs.name: Nightly OWL Alpha Dev Pipeline
jobs.prompt:
You are OWL Alpha, Sean's primary AI agent. Execute the nightly development pipeline.

## Projects (check each for pending work)

### 1. Hermes Web Computer (HWC)
- Source: /opt/data/hermes-web-computer/
- State: /opt/data/hermes-web-computer-state/
- Check PHASE_TRACKER.json for current phase
- phases 0-15 are complete (v1.4). If current_phase >= 15 and status == "complete":
  - Check docs/WAYBAR-SPEC.md for any remaining §12 open items
  - Check for v1.5 tasks or new feature requests
  - If nothing pending, report "HWC: no pending phases"
- If a phase is pending: implement it per the spec, run tests, commit, push

### 2. GTO Wizard Clone
- Source: /tmp/gto-wizard-clone/
- Check git log for recent activity
- Verify docker compose builds: cd /tmp/gto-wizard-clone && docker compose build
- Run tests: cd /tmp/gto-wizard-clone && npm test (if available)
- Fix any build or test failures
- Check if the app can start: docker compose up -d, curl localhost:3000
- Commit and push any fixes

### 3. Roadmap Autonomy Engine
- Source: /tmp/hermes-sync/ (copy from /opt/data/hermes-sync/ if needed)
- Check workspace/plans/roadmap.json for pending tasks
- Execute highest-priority pending task
- Update roadmap.json, commit, push

### 4. Hermes Agent
- Source: /opt/hermes/ (this repo)
- Check for any TODO/FIXME in code that can be quickly resolved
- Run tests if changes are made: cd /opt/hermes && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q

## Reporting
At the end, deliver a concise summary:
- What was checked
- What was done (commits, fixes, new features)
- What's still blocked
- Disk/memory status (df -h /opt/data, free -h)

Keep it actionable. If everything is clean, say so briefly.

jobs.name: LinkedIn Daily Post
jobs.prompt:
You are running the LinkedIn daily post pipeline. Execute these steps:

1. Read the linkedin-hooks skill at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/skills/linkedin-hooks/SKILL.md for hook formulas and post templates.

2. Generate a LinkedIn post draft (150-250 words) using one of the hook formulas. Rotate through these content pillars by day:
   - Monday: Build Log (what shipped this week)
   - Tuesday: Teach (explain a concept from your work)
   - Wednesday: Hot Take (opinion on AI infra / self-hosting)
   - Thursday: Community (shoutout or collab)
   - Friday: Build Log (weekly roundup)
   
   Focus on: Hermes WebUI, agent-os, HWC tiling desktop, TrueNAS self-hosting, Playwright automation, n8n workflows, Go/Svelte5 development.

3. Save the draft to /workspace/linkedin-drafts/YYYY-MM-DD.md with:
   - Hook used
   - Post text
   - Hashtags
   - Pillar type
   - Status: draft

4. Queue the post by running:
   python3 /workspace/linkedin-queue-post.py "POST_TEXT"
   (Replace POST_TEXT with the actual post text from step 2)

5. Confirm the post was queued. Do NOT try to run the Playwright bot directly.

Report: what post was drafted, which hook was used, and queue status.

jobs.name: LinkedIn Auto Post
jobs.prompt:
You are the LinkedIn auto-post runner. Execute these steps:

1. SSH to the host: ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no sean@172.19.0.1

2. Check if there are queued posts:
   ssh ... "python3 /workspace/linkedin-queue-post.py --next"

3. If a post is queued, SSH to host and run the post runner:
   ssh ... "cd /workspace && python3 /workspace/linkedin-post-runner.py"

4. Report the outcome: posted, skipped (no queue), or error.
If the bot reports session expired, notify that the LinkedIn session needs manual refresh (run linkedin-login.py on host).

jobs.name: LinkedIn Engagement
jobs.prompt:
You are the LinkedIn engagement bot runner. Execute these steps:

1. Load the linkedin-engagement skill at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/skills/linkedin-engagement/SKILL.md for comment templates.

2. SSH to host (172.19.0.1) and run the engagement bot:
   ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no sean@172.19.0.1
   
   a. First check session validity:
      python3 /workspace/linkedin-browser.py
   
   b. If session is valid, navigate to LinkedIn feed and comment on up to 5 posts:
      - Use Playwright to open https://www.linkedin.com/feed/
      - Find the first 5 posts with meaningful content
      - For each post, click Comment, type a unique value-adding comment using the engagement skill templates, submit
      - Wait 5-15 seconds between comments
      
   c. Then check notifications:
      - Open https://www.linkedin.com/notifications/
      - Reply to any comments on your posts (thank them + follow-up question)
      - Log all actions

3. Report: number of comments made, notifications handled, any action items.

jobs.name: LinkedIn Weekly Audit
jobs.prompt:
You are the LinkedIn weekly profile auditor. Generate a weekly LinkedIn audit report.

1. Audit profile completeness (score each section):
   - Headline: present? compelling? <120 chars?
   - About section: present? uses problem-solution-proof-CTA structure?
   - Featured section: 3 items from repos?
   - Skills section: top 3 pinned? 50 skills listed?
   - Profile photo: present?
   - Banner image: present?
   - Creator mode: enabled?

2. Check activity data (SSH to host 172.19.0.1):
   a. Posts this week: cat /home/sean/n8n-data/linkedin-post-queue.jsonl | grep posted
   b. Engagement log: tail -50 /home/sean/n8n-data/linkedin-engagement-log.jsonl
   c. Bot log: tail -30 /home/sean/n8n-data/linkedin-bot.log
   d. Session health: python3 /workspace/linkedin-browser.py (verify session valid)

3. Write audit report to /workspace/linkedin-audit/weekly-YYYY-MM-DD.md:
   - Profile completeness score (0-100%)
   - Content metrics (posts made, engagement rate)
   - 3 specific action items for next week
   - Session health status

4. DO NOT make any profile changes. Only report.

Report: summary score, key findings, 3 action items.
